comments.body:
Hi @ritesh-1918 ,

IÔÇÖd like to work on this issue.

IÔÇÖll run:

```bash id="upb5jt"
npm run lint
```

inside the frontend directory and systematically fix the reported linting problems, including:

* unused variables/imports
* missing React hook dependencies
* formatting inconsistencies
* minor TypeScript/ESLint warnings

IÔÇÖll ensure the fixes are clean, non-breaking, and consistent with the existing codebase style before raising a PR.

Please assign this issue to me. Thanks! Ô¡É­ƒÆø

Hi @sriram687, welcome to the project! ­ƒÜÇ 

I've marked you down to take the lead on this issue. (I tried assigning you formally via GitHub, but since you are not a collaborator on this repo yet, it didn't allow it. You are good to go ahead in spirit!)

Before you dive into the code, you'll need to set up and access the application to test your changes. Here is the step-by-step guide to get access to the dashboard:

### ­ƒÜÇ Contributor Onboarding Guide
1. **Create an Account:** Go to the sign-up page on your local build (usually http://localhost:5173/signup) or our deployed link.
2. **Select the Company:** During sign-up, you MUST select **Ritesh Private Limited** as the company.
3. **Verify Your Email:** Complete the email verification step first.
4. **Request Access:** After your email is confirmed, you need manual approval to access the user dashboard. You can either:
   - Send me a formal WhatsApp message requesting access.
   - Or, simply reply here on GitHub with the email address you used to sign up.
   - Alternatively, you can reply here if you have any questions!
5. **Get Approved:** Once I receive your request, I will approve your account from the backend. You will then have full access to the user perspective to reproduce bugs and test your features!

Happy coding! We're looking forward to your PR. Let me know when you've signed up!

Hi @ritesh-1918 ,
I would like to work on this issue under GSSoC 2026.
IÔÇÖll run the linter in the frontend, fix the reported warnings/errors (unused variables, missing hook dependencies, formatting issues, etc.), ensure the app works properly after cleanup, and then raise a PR.

Hi @sriram687, 

I saw your updated comment with the detailed step-by-step plan! That looks like a very solid and systematic approach to clearing out the frontend linting errors (unused variables, hook dependencies, formatting, and warnings). 

Please go ahead and work on this planÔÇölet's make sure we come up with a clean, high-quality fix for it. No worries at all on this side. Happy coding, and looking forward to your PR!
Hi @vasheekhan, thank you for your interest! As noted above, @sriram687 is already assigned and leading the work on this issue. Please feel free to check out other open issues in the repository!

title: [CHORE] Fix Frontend ESLint warnings and unused variables
